skills.name: deploy
skills.description: Deploy the project to staging or production
skills.prompt: Deploy Astro Maya to the specified environment. Ask the user if they want to deploy to 'staging' or 'production', then execute: git push origin main:staging (for staging) or git push origin main (for production). Wait for confirmation before proceeding.

skills.name: db-query
skills.prompt: Run a SQL query against the Supabase database. The user should provide the query. Use the Supabase MCP if authenticated, otherwise use the SQL editor in the Supabase dashboard. Common tables: profiles, projects, project_tasks, lesson_progress, user_xp_log, streaks, skills, user_skills, languages, lessons.

skills.name: add-lesson
skills.description: Add a new lesson to a language course
skills.prompt:
Create a new lesson for one of the Maya languages. Steps:
1. Ask which language (kiche, qeqchi, kaqchikel, mam, tzutujil)
2. Ask for lesson title, level (principiante/intermedio/avanzado), and description
3. Ask for vocabulary items (kiche/spanish pairs), phrases, grammar notes, cultural notes
4. Generate the lesson content JSON
5. Show the user the SQL INSERT statement to add to the database
6. Optionally create the lesson content in the corresponding content file if needed

args.name: language
args.description: Language slug (kiche, qeqchi, kaqchikel, mam, tzutujil)

skills.name: add-skill
skills.description: Add a new skill/badge to the system
skills.prompt:
Create a new skill/badge for users to earn. Steps:
1. Ask for skill name, description, and icon (emoji)
2. Ask which language it belongs to (or if it's global)
3. Ask for requirements to unlock it
4. Generate SQL INSERT for skills table
5. Show example code of how to award this skill in the app

skills.name: user-stats
skills.prompt:
Query user statistics from the database. Ask for user email or ID, then show:
- Profile info
- Total XP
- Current streak
- Projects count
- Lessons completed
- Skills earned
Use Supabase queries to get this data from profiles, user_xp_log, streaks, projects, lesson_progress, and user_skills tables.

skills.name: improve-ux
skills.description: Implement UX improvements following Astro Maya standards
skills.prompt:
Implement specific UX improvements for Astro Maya. The user should specify which feature to implement from this list:

1. SKELETON-LOADERS: Replace 'Cargando...' texts with animated skeleton loaders using shimmer effect in dashboard, projects, and lesson pages.

2. EMPTY-STATES: Replace empty state messages with illustrated empty states including: illustration/emoji, friendly message, and clear CTA button. Apply to projects list, skills grid, lessons list.

3. ONBOARDING: Create a 3-step onboarding modal for new users: (1) Welcome to Astro Maya, (2) Create your first project, (3) Complete your first lesson. Use localStorage to show only once.

4. TOAST-NOTIFICATIONS: Implement toast notification system using Sonner or custom. Show toasts for: XP earned, skill unlocked, streak updated, lesson completed, project completed. Auto-dismiss after 4 seconds.

5. CELEBRATIONS: Add confetti animation (canvas-confetti) when: completing a lesson, completing a project, earning a skill. Trigger from project detail page completeTask function.

6. AUDIO-PLAYER: Add audio playback for vocabulary and phrases. Create AudioButton component that plays TTS or audio files. Position next to each word/phrase in lesson content.

7. INTERACTIVE-QUIZ: Replace 'Ver respuesta' spoilers with interactive quiz components: multiple choice, text input with validation, matching pairs. Show correct/incorrect feedback immediately.

8. STREAK-REMINDER: Add streak warning when user hasn't studied in 20+ hours. Show banner in dashboard with '¡Tu racha de X días está en riesgo!' CTA to continue learning.

9. SEARCH-FILTER: Add search bar and filters to projects page. Filter by: language, status (active/completed), progress percentage. Search by project title.

10. ACCESSIBILITY: Add ARIA labels to all interactive elements, ensure keyboard navigation works, verify color contrast meets WCAG 2.1 AA standards.

Ask the user which number(s) to implement, then create the necessary components and integrate them. Always test with npm run build after changes.

args.name: feature
args.description: UX feature number(s) to implement (1-10, comma separated for multiple)

skills.name: fix-responsive
skills.description: Fix responsive design issues in a component
skills.prompt:
Analyze and fix responsive design issues in a specific component. Ask which component/file to fix, then:
1. Read the file
2. Identify mobile layout issues
3. Apply Tailwind responsive classes (sm:, md:, lg:)
4. Ensure proper text wrapping (break-words)
5. Check grid layouts for mobile
6. Build and verify changes

skills.name: content-check
skills.description: Check content files for missing translations or data
skills.prompt:
Review the lesson content files (kicheLeccion.js, qeqchiLeccion.js, etc.) for:
1. Missing Spanish translations
2. Incomplete lessons
3. Missing vocabulary or phrases
4. Grammar notes
5. Cultural notes
Provide a summary of what's complete vs missing.

skills.name: test-build
skills.description: Run build and check for errors
skills.prompt: Execute npm run build to verify the project compiles without errors. If there are TypeScript errors, ESLint warnings, or build failures, analyze and fix them. Show the final build output summary.
